title: Qualys Policy Compliance (PC) API — Posture, Controls, Scan Results

confirmed_facts.fact: PC API base: /api/2.0/fo/compliance/ — same auth and platform URL as VMDR classic APIs (Basic auth, form-encoded POST, XML response)
List compliance posture: POST /api/2.0/fo/compliance/posture/info/?action=list — returns host-level compliance status per policy
Posture info fields: HOST_ID, IP, DNS, POLICY_ID, POLICY_TITLE, STATUS (Pass/Fail), CRITICALITY, CONTROLS_TOTAL, CONTROLS_PASSED, CONTROLS_FAILED
List compliance scan results: POST /api/2.0/fo/scan/compliance/?action=list — returns scan metadata (ref, title, date, status, type)
Fetch compliance scan results: POST /api/2.0/fo/scan/compliance/?action=fetch&scan_ref=scan/xxx — returns per-host per-control results
List policies: POST /api/2.0/fo/compliance/policy/?action=list — returns policy ID, title, creation/modification dates
Control list: POST /api/2.0/fo/compliance/control/?action=list — returns CID, statement, criticality, technology. Supports filtering by ids, technology, criticality.
Posture info supports scoping parameters: policy_id, host_ids, ip, asset_group_ids, compliance_enabled (1), status (Passed/Failed/Error)
PC scan types: On-demand, Scheduled, API-launched. Launch compliance scan: POST /api/2.0/fo/scan/compliance/?action=launch with scan_title, option_id, ip/asset_group_ids
Exception list: POST /api/2.0/fo/compliance/exception/?action=list — returns approved exceptions with control ID, host, expiry, reason

implementation_notes:
Get compliance posture for a policy:
```python
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def get_compliance_posture(session, base_url, policy_id=None, status=None):
    url = f'{base_url}/api/2.0/fo/compliance/posture/info/'
    params = {'action': 'list', 'compliance_enabled': '1'}
    if policy_id:
        params['policy_id'] = policy_id
    if status:
        params['status'] = status  # 'Passed', 'Failed', 'Error'
    
    resp = session.post(url, data=params, timeout=(10, 300))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    root = ET.fromstring(resp.text)
    
    rows = []
    for info in root.findall('.//POSTURE_INFO'):
        rows.append({
            'host_id': info.findtext('HOST_ID', ''),
            'ip': info.findtext('IP', ''),
            'dns': info.findtext('DNS', ''),
            'policy_id': info.findtext('POLICY_ID', ''),
            'policy_title': info.findtext('POLICY_TITLE', ''),
            'status': info.findtext('STATUS', ''),
            'controls_total': info.findtext('CONTROLS_TOTAL', '0'),
            'controls_passed': info.findtext('CONTROLS_PASSED', '0'),
            'controls_failed': info.findtext('CONTROLS_FAILED', '0'),
        })
    return rows
```

List all policies:
```python
def list_policies(session, base_url):
    url = f'{base_url}/api/2.0/fo/compliance/policy/'
    resp = session.post(url, data={'action': 'list'}, timeout=(10, 60))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    root = ET.fromstring(resp.text)
    return [{
        'id': p.findtext('ID', ''),
        'title': p.findtext('TITLE', ''),
    } for p in root.findall('.//POLICY')]
```

Calculate compliance percentage per policy:
```python
def compliance_summary(posture_rows):
    from collections import defaultdict
    policies = defaultdict(lambda: {'total': 0, 'passed': 0, 'failed': 0})
    for row in posture_rows:
        pid = row['policy_id']
        policies[pid]['title'] = row['policy_title']
        policies[pid]['total'] += int(row['controls_total'] or 0)
        policies[pid]['passed'] += int(row['controls_passed'] or 0)
        policies[pid]['failed'] += int(row['controls_failed'] or 0)
    
    summary = []
    for pid, data in policies.items():
        total = data['total']
        pct = (data['passed'] / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0
        summary.append({
            'policy_id': pid,
            'policy_title': data['title'],
            'total_controls': total,
            'passed': data['passed'],
            'failed': data['failed'],
            'compliance_pct': round(pct, 1),
        })
    return summary
```

Fetch detailed scan results for a specific scan:
```python
def get_scan_results(session, base_url, scan_ref):
    url = f'{base_url}/api/2.0/fo/scan/compliance/'
    params = {'action': 'fetch', 'scan_ref': scan_ref, 'output_format': 'CSV_NO_METADATA'}
    resp = session.post(url, data=params, timeout=(10, 300))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    import csv, io
    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    return list(reader)
```
caveats: PC APIs use the same auth and form-encoded POST as VMDR — same session and platform URL
Posture info can be very large for subscriptions with many policies and hosts — always filter by policy_id or status
Compliance scans must be explicitly configured for hosts — hosts not in a PC scan profile will have no posture data
The 'status' filter values are case-sensitive: 'Passed' not 'passed'
PC requires a separate Qualys subscription/module — not all Qualys customers have PC enabled
Exceptions (approved deviations) are NOT reflected in the posture pass/fail counts — check the exception list separately for accurate reporting
